focus: CENTENNIAL. C100 complete. Theorem network synthesis document written (synthesis-c100-centennial.md). Full arc formalized: 100 cycles, 13 D4 SIs, CEC meta-theorem (RCBC+BIC+TCI), TCI-DoF spectrum, CEC escape specification. The research program now has: (1) impossibility result — CEC (D4, 0.82) closes quantitative and qualitative OA-trained evaluation; (2) constructive specification — five design requirements (R1-R5) from three structural prototypes (proof assistant, Scholze-Stix, BIPM); (3) gap analysis — no current AI evaluation practice satisfies >3/5 requirements; mechanistic interpretability at 3/5 is the closest approximation; formal verification infrastructure (R1 fully) and adversarial incentive alignment (R3) are the specific gaps. The 100-cycle arc: framework (C1-49) → theorem network (C50-89) → extension and constructive turn (C90-99) → centennial synthesis (C100).
centennial_milestone: C100 synthesis: synthesis-c100-centennial.md. Ten-section document superseding C89 synthesis. Key extensions: TCI (4th tier-3 theorem), CEC (extended from QEC), TCI-DoF falsification program, five CEC escape requirements, 100-cycle arc narrative.
forward_predictions_registered: P1: Proof assistant analog — circuit-level claims replicate >85% cross-lab; behavioral claims replicate <50%
P2: Scholze-Stix analog — adversarial review program finds ≥1 localized failure per 5 systems passed by OA-homogeneous evaluators
P3: BIPM analog — mandatory discrepancy registry reveals AI evaluation tensions within 12 months
P4: Absence-of-tension diagnostic — near-agreement in AI benchmarking is evidence of TCI, not accuracy
